title: filago arvensis subsp. lagopus
intro: Filago arvensis subsp. lagopus, also known as corn cleavers, is a subspecies of the filago arvensis plant, a type of flowering plant in the daisy family. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other compounds that exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, making it a valuable resource for medicinal purposes. The health benefits of corn cleavers include its ability to help soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions. Corn cleavers can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the skin.
